title: Implement Pillow grid assembly with slide number labels
description: In thumbnails.py, implement assemble_grid() that accepts a list of PIL Images and a column count, arranges them in row-major order, and annotates each cell with its 1-based slide number label. Returns a single PIL Image.
acceptance_criteria: assemble_grid() accepts a list of PIL Images and an integer cols argument
Images are arranged in row-major order with no more than cols columns per row
Each cell is annotated with its 1-based slide number
The function returns a single PIL Image ready for saving

handoff_summary.completed: assemble_grid() implemented: row-major layout, 1-based slide number labels, white text with dark drop-shadow, Pillow-version-safe font loading, empty-list guard. File compiles cleanly.
handoff_summary.risks: Cell dimensions are taken from images[0].size; if callers pass images of varying sizes, cells in later rows will be pasted at incorrect offsets. generate_thumbnails() always produces uniform sizes so this is safe within the current pipeline.
handoff_summary.next_action: Hand off to tester agent for validation of assemble_grid() behaviour (row-major order, label values, output dimensions).
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: None — assemble_grid() is a new function added between _make_hatched_placeholder and generate_thumbnails; no existing code modified.
handoff_summary.design_decisions: Used ImageFont.load_default(size=) with TypeError fallback to avoid any external font file dependency. Cell size derived from images[0].size, consistent with generate_thumbnails which always produces uniform dimensions. Drop-shadow (1px offset dark text under white text) chosen over a filled rectangle to keep implementation simple and avoid RGBA conversion.
handoff_summary.test_coverage_notes: Tester should cover: empty list guard, single image, exact multiple of cols, non-multiple cols (partial last row), label values (1-based, correct per position), output pixel dimensions.
handoff_summary.known_limitations: assemble_grid() assumes all images are the same size (uses images[0].size as the canonical cell dimension). Heterogeneous sizes would produce misaligned pastes. This is acceptable given the current pipeline always produces uniform thumbnails.

conflict_risks: None — assemble_grid() is a new function added between _make_hatched_placeholder and generate_thumbnails; no existing code modified.

execution_history.summary: Implemented assemble_grid() in pypptx/ops/thumbnails.py. The function accepts a list of PIL Images and an integer cols, arranges them in row-major order using divmod, annotates each cell with its 1-based slide number (white text + dark drop-shadow for contrast), and returns a single PIL Image. Font is loaded via ImageFont.load_default(size=...) with a TypeError fallback for Pillow < 10. File passes py_compile with no errors.

guardrails.template_text:
# Developer Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Implement only the assigned bead inside the existing architecture and file scope.

Allowed actions:
- Modify code, configuration, or local docs required to complete the assigned bead.
- Create sub-beads for discovered follow-up work that should be handled separately.
- Verify your `Python` changes compile and import correctly with a quick syntax check: `python -m py_compile`.

Disallowed actions:
- Run any test suite — not even a targeted `pytest` run or any subset. Test execution is exclusively the tester agent's responsibility. Running tests wastes the agent budget and delays the pipeline.
- Redesign unrelated architecture or broaden scope beyond the assigned bead.
- Perform final review signoff in place of a review agent.
- Silently absorb unrelated test, documentation, or planning work that should be handed off.

Expected outputs:
- Completed or blocked JSON with concise implementation summary.
- Accurate touched files, changed files, risks, and follow-up handoff fields.
